column1: # Known SAP stuck-points, consulted by sap-gui-skill-scaffold Step 0.9 (--goal).
# Parsed by Claude (Read tool), NOT by VBS. Tab-separated with REAL tab bytes.
# Skip rule: ignore blank lines and lines beginning with '#'. Split each data
# row on TAB into the 8 columns documented below.
#   1 txn            SAP transaction code, uppercase (SE11, SE38, SE37, ...).
#   2 object_type    Catalog key the object discriminator maps to
#                     SEARCHHELP, LOCKOBJECT, PROGRAM, FM, CLASS, INTERFACE),
#                    or '*' for traps that apply to every object in that txn.
#   3 trap_name      Short identifier for the stuck-point (feeds the mode label).
#   4 scenario_type  One of the 5 probe taxonomy values: success / not_found /
#   5 applies        Selection gate. One of: always | object_type in (A,B,C) |
#                    Step 0.9.3 evaluates this and skips non-matching rows.
#   6 probe_hint     The flow spine Claude turns into the probe scenario text.
#   7 value_hint     Concrete/templated test value when --goal supplies none.
#                    A leading '(' marks a non-probe note (no scenario emitted).
#   8 source         Provenance (memory-file stem or skill SKILL.md section).
# MAINTENANCE (append-on-discovery): when a --goal run's probe surfaces a popup
# or error this catalog did not predict for a txn+object, Step 5 self-review
# emits a CATALOG-CANDIDATE finding. A human reviews and hand-adds the row here
# (manual edit only, per CLAUDE.md Directive 2 — no script rewrites this file).
# Mirror new shipped-skill Known Limitations into this catalog in the same PR.

column6: create a domain with DATATYPE CHAR length 10, save as local object ($TMP), activate, then F3 back to the entry screen
display a domain that does not exist; expect a "does not exist" message at the SE11 entry screen
create a domain DATATYPE CHAR with a LENGTH far over the type maximum; expect a field-validation rejection
delete an existing domain; watch for the SAPLSETX original/logon-language popup and confirm the TADIR row is actually removed afterwards
create a transparent table with MANDT plus one data-element field, set technical settings + delivery class, set enhancement category, activate
create + activate a Z table/structure with NO enhancement category preset; expect the "not classified / enhancement category" dialog mid-activate
press Create on the DATATYPE radio; expect the sub-type selection popup (Structure / Data element / Table type) — DOMAIN never shows it
add a field by typing DATATYPE+LENGTH directly with no data element; expect the read-only-type abort (DD03D-DATATYPE Changeable=False)
attempt an SE11 delete of a database table; expect "no delete function" — SE14 / RS_DD_TABDEL is the real path
column8: sap-se11/SKILL.md "SE11 Has No Delete Function for Tables"

column6: after activate, confirm the object is ACTIVE via the post-activate RFC verify; a status-bar MessageType E must fail-closed, not report false success
create a minimal report, paste source, syntax check (Ctrl+F2), activate (Ctrl+F3), verify the active state
display a program that does not exist; expect not-found at the SE38 entry screen
delete a program whose master language differs from the logon language; expect the SAPLSETX original/logon-language popup — choose "maintain in original language", then confirm PROGDIR/TADIR are gone
activate then re-open; screen 101 is ambiguous — accept success only when the status-bar MessageType is not E/A
create an RFC-enabled function module in an existing function group, paste the source, activate
create an FM when the install master language differs from the logon language; expect the "object maintenance only possible in original language" info popup — press Continue, the FM is still created
display a function module that does not exist
activate an FM; the inactive-objects worklist popup offers Continue only (no Select-All) — pressing Continue with nothing selected hangs
